scheme_name: Deen Dayal Upadhyay Kaushal Kendra (DDUKK)- Mega Skill Centre
page_title: Deen Dayal Upadhyay Kaushal Kendra (DDUKK)- Mega Skill Centre

details: The “Deen Dayal Upadhyay Kaushal Kendra (DDUKK)—Mega Skill Centre” is a component of the umbrella scheme “Mukhymantri Sarthi Yojna” launched by the Department of Labour, Employment, Training and Skill Development, Jharkhand. The scheme, operational since October 2017, focuses on longer-duration courses with on-job training (OJT) for youth. It aims to train 60,000 candidates in various sectors during 2023-24.
The training under this scheme is a minimum of 576 hours of which 160 hours are dedicated to IT and Soft Skills
benefits: Combines technical skills with soft skills and IT training.
Training is fully residential, ensuring a focused learning environment.
Hands-on industry exposure for candidates to become job-ready.

Tourism & Hospitality, Telecom, Security, Retail, Plumbing, Logistics, IT/ITES, Electronics, Domestic Worker, Construction, Capital Goods, BFSI, Beauty & Wellness, Automotive, Apparel, and Agriculture, Food Processing, Healthcare, Iron & Steel, Mining
eligibility: The applicant must be a resident of Jharkhand.
The applicant's age should be 18-35 years.
The applicant must belong to one of the priority groups (SC/ST, women, PwD, transgender).

online: for registration under the umbrella scheme "Mukhymantri Sarthi Yojna".
Candidate registration would be Aadhaar-based & hence Aadhaar number at the time of candidate registration would be mandatory.

faqs.question: What is Deen Dayal Upadhyay Kaushal Kendra?
faqs.answer: A scheme to provide skill training with on-job training under a PPP model.

faqs.question: Who can apply for the training?
faqs.answer: Unemployed youth aged 18-35 years, meeting the educational and residency criteria.

faqs.question: What sectors are covered under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Over 20 sectors, including IT/ITES, Healthcare, Automotive, Agriculture, and Tourism.

faqs.question: Is the training residential?
faqs.answer: Yes, all training is fully residential.

faqs.question: What is the duration of the training?
faqs.answer: Minimum 576 hours, with 160 hours for IT and soft skills.

faqs.question: How many candidates will be trained annually?
faqs.answer: The target is 60,000 candidates per year.

faqs.question: What is the infrastructure requirement for the training centers?
faqs.answer: A minimum of 25,000 sq. ft. area for training and hostels.

faqs.question: Are there any fees for the training?
faqs.answer: No, the training is free for eligible candidates.

faqs.question: What is On Job Training (OJT)?
faqs.answer: Practical, hands-on training in real industry environments to prepare candidates for jobs.

faqs.question: How can I apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Apply online through JSDMS or at designated centers or online.
